svg.aria-label: The Reflexion loop drawn as a cycle of four boxes. An actor acts on the task and reads the episodic buffer first. An external checker, RAG Part 11's judge repurposed as a pass or fail reward signal against the policy, grades the attempt. On a pass the loop converges. On a fail, a reflector turns the verdict into a concrete verbal lesson, which is appended to the episodic buffer, and the next trial reads it before acting. The lesson is plain text, no weights are updated, which is why this is called verbal reinforcement.
svg.title: The Reflexion loop as verbal reinforcement: actor, checker, reflector, episodic buffer

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: The Reflexion loop: a verdict on a failure becomes a verbal lesson the next trial reads

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: actor → checker (reward) → self-reflection (verbal) → buffer → actor. Nothing is retrained; the policy update is a sentence in a prompt.

svg.text: The lesson is plain text in a buffer, not a weight update. This is verbal reinforcement: the policy improves by what the next trial reads, not by a gradient step.
